geographic_scope: Main invasion corridors in northern Vietnam
theater_description: The short Chinese invasion of northern Vietnam across the main frontier corridors during the formal February-March 1979 war window.
geometry_method_note: Episode is limited to the main invasion window and principal frontier corridors rather than the wider 1980s border war.
military_area_note: Combatant exposure uses actually committed Chinese forces and Vietnamese defenders in the invasion theater, excluding national armed-force totals and later reservist mobilization outside the episode.
civilian_area_note: No publishable civilian exposure denominator was recovered for the main invasion front as a whole.
split_reason: Separated from later clashes because the 1979 invasion was short, high-intensity, and operationally distinct from later artillery duels and border incidents.
quality_note: Combatant deaths and combatant exposure can be bounded, but civilian direct-death totals and civilian exposure remain too weak for a full DR.

geographic_scope: Tong Chup village / pig farm site
theater_description: A one-day mass killing of civilians at Tong Chup during the withdrawal phase of the 1979 invasion.
geometry_method_note: Bounded narrowly to the massacre site and date, not generalized to all civilian victimization in the invasion.
military_area_note: No combatant denominator or combatant death series was recovered for the same one-day micro-episode.
civilian_area_note: Civilian exposure denominator for the pig farm group / village population was not recovered.
split_reason: Separated because the event has a clear civilian direct-death count but is too narrow and asymmetric to support a full DR.
quality_note: Direct civilian deaths are well corroborated, but no matched combatant data or denominator exists.
